rows.text: If earthly fathers give good gifts, how much more will the heavenly Father give good things to those who ask
rows.quotePreview: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heav…

rows.text: Call no man your father on earth — you have one Father, who is in heaven
rows.quotePreview: And call no man your father upon the earth: for one is your Father, which is in heaven.

rows.text: God is Spirit — those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth; the Father seeks such worshipers
rows.quotePreview: But the hour cometh, and now is, when the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth: for the Fath…

rows.text: I AM the good shepherd — I know my sheep and lay down my life for them
rows.quotePreview: I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep. I am the good shepherd, and know my sheep, and …

rows.text: I AM the true vine, and my Father is the vinedresser
rows.quotePreview: I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman. I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in…

rows.text: Before Abraham was, I AM
rows.quotePreview: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Before Abraham was, I am.

rows.text: I AM the Alpha and the Omega, the root and offspring of David, the bright morning star
rows.quotePreview: I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end, the first and the last. I Jesus have sent mine angel to testify unto yo…

rows.text: All who came before him are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not hear them
rows.quotePreview: All that ever came before me are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.

rows.text: The hireling, who is not the shepherd, sees the wolf coming and flees because he does not own the sheep, leaving them to be caught and scattered
rows.quotePreview: But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sh…

rows.text: Urges the crowd to walk and believe while the light is with them, so they may become children of light
rows.quotePreview: Yet a little while is the light with you. Walk while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon you: for he that walketh…

rows.text: I and the Father are one — the Father is in me and I am in the Father
rows.quotePreview: I and my Father are one. But if I do, though ye believe not me, believe the works: that ye may know, and believe, that t…

rows.text: The Father loves the Son and has given all things into his hand — all things have been delivered to me; no one knows the Son except the Father
Tells the disciples that to know him is to know the Father — and from now on they have seen the Father
rows.quotePreview: If ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also: and from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.

rows.text: This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears
rows.quotePreview: This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears.

rows.text: He came from the Father into the world and now returns to the Father
rows.quotePreview: I came forth from the Father, and am come into the world: again, I leave the world, and go to the Father.

rows.text: Came from heaven to do the Father's will — to lose none given to him but raise them up at the last day
rows.quotePreview: For I came down from heaven, not to do mine own will, but the will of him that sent me. And this is the Father’s will wh…

rows.subcategoryTitle: Predictions of His Suffering, Death, and Resurrection
rows.text: Predicts his passion for the third time — the Son of man shall be betrayed, condemned, mocked, scourged, crucified, and the third day rise again
rows.quotePreview: Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be betrayed unto the chief priests and unto the scribes, and the…

rows.subcategoryTitle: Predictions of His Suffering, Death, and Resurrection
rows.text: Announces that the hour for the Son of Man to be glorified has come
rows.quotePreview: The hour is come, that the Son of man should be glorified.

rows.text: Promises Nathanael will see greater things — heaven open and angels ascending and descending on the Son of Man
rows.quotePreview: Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig tree, believest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these. Veril…

rows.text: Jesus assures the repentant criminal crucified beside him that on that very day he will be with him in paradise, offering immediate salvation to one who turns to him in his final moments.
rows.quotePreview: Verily I say unto thee, To day shalt thou be with me in paradise.

rows.text: From the cross Jesus entrusts his mother to the care of the beloved disciple, saying to her 'Woman, behold your son' and to the disciple 'Behold your mother,' establishing a new family bond at the moment of his death.
rows.quotePreview: Woman, behold thy son! Behold thy mother!

rows.text: At the ninth hour Jesus cries out with a loud voice the opening words of Psalm 22 — 'My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?' — giving voice to the full weight of divine abandonment he bears as the sin-bearer on the cross.
rows.quotePreview: Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?

rows.text: Jesus declares 'I thirst' from the cross, fulfilling Scripture and expressing the full physical humanity of the Son of God in his atoning suffering.
rows.quotePreview: I thirst.

rows.text: Jesus cries out 'It is finished' (tetelestai) as his final public word before death, declaring the complete accomplishment of his atoning work and the full discharge of every obligation his mission required.
rows.quotePreview: It is finished:

rows.text: With a loud voice Jesus entrusts his spirit to the Father in the words of Psalm 31:5, committing himself to God's care as he breathes his last and demonstrating that his death is a voluntary, trusting surrender into the Father's hands.
rows.quotePreview: Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit:

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Promise and Coming of the Spirit
rows.text: Promises not to leave them as orphans — he will come to them, and those who keep his commandments will be loved by the Father
rows.quotePreview: I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you. Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me:…

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Spirit's Work in the World
rows.text: The Spirit will convict the world of sin (unbelief), righteousness (Jesus's return to the Father), and judgment (the ruler of this world condemned)
rows.quotePreview: And when he is come, he will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment: Of sin, because they belie…

rows.subcategoryTitle: Nature and Character of the Kingdom
rows.text: Kingdom compared to treasure hidden in a field, and to a merchant who sells everything for one pearl of great value — worth giving up everything
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field; the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for…

rows.subcategoryTitle: Nature and Character of the Kingdom
rows.text: Kingdom of heaven compared to ten virgins awaiting the bridegroom — be ready
rows.quotePreview: Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins, which took their lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegr…

rows.text: Enter by the narrow gate — broad and easy is the way to destruction; few find the narrow way
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Enter ye in at the strait gate: for wide is the gate, and broad is the way, that leadeth to destruction, and many ther…

rows.text: It is hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom — easier for a camel through a needle's eye
rows.quotePreview: Verily I say unto you, That a rich man shall hardly enter into the kingdom of heaven. And again I say unto you, It is ea…

rows.subcategoryTitle: Citizens of the Kingdom — The Beatitudes
rows.text: Blessed are the poor in spirit — theirs is the kingdom of heaven
rows.quotePreview: Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

rows.subcategoryTitle: Citizens of the Kingdom — The Beatitudes
rows.text: Blessed are those who mourn — they shall be comforted
rows.quotePreview: Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.

rows.subcategoryTitle: Citizens of the Kingdom — The Beatitudes
rows.text: Blessed are the meek — they shall inherit the earth
rows.quotePreview: Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.

rows.subcategoryTitle: Citizens of the Kingdom — The Beatitudes
rows.text: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness — they shall be satisfied
rows.quotePreview: Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.

rows.subcategoryTitle: Citizens of the Kingdom — The Beatitudes
rows.text: Blessed are the merciful — they shall receive mercy
rows.quotePreview: Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.

rows.subcategoryTitle: Citizens of the Kingdom — The Beatitudes
rows.text: Blessed are the pure in heart — they shall see God
rows.quotePreview: Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.

rows.subcategoryTitle: Citizens of the Kingdom — The Beatitudes
rows.text: Blessed are the peacemakers — they shall be called sons of God
rows.quotePreview: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.

rows.subcategoryTitle: Citizens of the Kingdom — The Beatitudes
rows.text: Blessed are those persecuted for righteousness — theirs is the kingdom; rejoice, great is the reward in heaven
rows.quotePreview: Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness’ sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. Blessed are ye, when…

rows.text: There is more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than ninety-nine who need no repentance
rows.quotePreview: I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just…

rows.text: Pronounces woe on Chorazin and Bethsaida — Tyre and Sidon would have repented in sackcloth and ashes had they seen such mighty works
rows.quotePreview: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works, which were done in you, had been done in Tyr…

rows.text: Come to me, all who are weary and burdened — take my yoke and learn from me; I am gentle and lowly; my yoke is easy and burden light
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn of me…

rows.text: Entry into the kingdom requires being born again — of water and Spirit; flesh births flesh, Spirit births spirit
rows.quotePreview: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God. Verily, verily, I say unt…

rows.subcategoryTitle: Eternal Life — Its Nature and Assurance
rows.text: Defines eternal life — knowing the only true God and Jesus Christ whom he sent
rows.quotePreview: And this is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast sent.

rows.subcategoryTitle: Eternal Life — Its Nature and Assurance
rows.text: My sheep hear my voice — I know them, they follow me, I give them eternal life; no one can snatch them from my hand
rows.quotePreview: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me: And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never per…

rows.text: Enter by the narrow gate — broad and easy is the way to destruction; the narrow way leads to life and few find it
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Enter ye in at the strait gate: for wide is the gate, and broad is the way, that leadeth to destruction, and many ther…

rows.text: Rebukes the disciples' little faith — do they not remember the loaves of the five thousand and the four thousand?
rows.quotePreview: O ye of little faith, why reason ye among yourselves, because ye have brought no bread? Do ye not yet understand, neithe…

rows.text: He has not come to abolish the Law but to fulfill it — not one jot or tittle will pass; whoever relaxes the least commandment will be least in the kingdom
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Think not that I am come to destroy the law, or the prophets: I am not come to destroy, but to fulfil. For verily I sa…

rows.text: Commands Satan to leave — worship the Lord your God and serve him only
rows.quotePreview: Get thee hence, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.

rows.subcategoryTitle: Moses and the Scriptures Pointing to Jesus
rows.text: He defends his Sabbath healing by appealing to the lawful practice of circumcision on the Sabbath, asking why they are angry that he made a man whole
rows.quotePreview: I have done one work, and ye all marvel. Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision; (not because it is of Moses, but of…

rows.text: This cup is my blood of the covenant — the new covenant in my blood, poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins; do this in remembrance of me
rows.quotePreview: For this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many for the remission of sins.

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Bread of Life — A Connected Teaching
rows.text: Whoever eats his flesh and drinks his blood has eternal life — his flesh is true food, his blood is true drink; they abide in him and he in them
rows.quotePreview: Whoso eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day. For my flesh i…

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Lord's Prayer — Pattern for Prayer
rows.text: Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name
rows.quotePreview: After this manner therefore pray ye: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name.

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Lord's Prayer — Pattern for Prayer
rows.text: Your kingdom come, your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven
rows.quotePreview: Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done in earth, as it is in heaven.

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Lord's Prayer — Pattern for Prayer
rows.text: Give us this day our daily bread
rows.quotePreview: Give us this day our daily bread.

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Lord's Prayer — Pattern for Prayer
rows.text: Forgive us our debts / sins as we also forgive our debtors
rows.quotePreview: And forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Lord's Prayer — Pattern for Prayer
rows.text: Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil
rows.quotePreview: And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil: For thine is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory, for e…

rows.text: Ask and it will be given; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened — everyone who asks receives
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you: For every one that as…

rows.text: Promises a coming day when he will speak plainly about the Father — and they will ask in his name
rows.quotePreview: These things have I spoken unto you in proverbs: but the time cometh, when I shall no more speak unto you in proverbs, b…

rows.text: Abide in him as the branch in the vine — apart from him you can do nothing; whoever does not abide withers and is burned
rows.quotePreview: Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, excep…

rows.text: Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you, pray for those who persecute you — be merciful as the Father is merciful
rows.quotePreview: But I say unto you, Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which…

rows.text: A new commandment: love one another as I have loved you — by this all will know you are his disciples
rows.quotePreview: A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another. By this…

rows.text: Greater love has no one than this: to lay down his life for his friends; you are his friends if you do what he commands
rows.quotePreview: Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends. Ye are my friends, if ye do whatsoever…

rows.text: If you love me, keep my commandments.
rows.quotePreview: ¶ If ye love me, keep my commandments.

rows.text: You are the light of the world — a city on a hill cannot be hidden; let your light shine so others may glorify the Father
rows.quotePreview: Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid. Neither do men light a candle, and put it un…

rows.text: He has not come to abolish the Law but to fulfill it — not one jot or tittle will pass; whoever relaxes even the least commandment will be least in the kingdom
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Think not that I am come to destroy the law, or the prophets: I am not come to destroy, but to fulfil. For verily I sa…

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Antitheses — “You Have Heard… But I Say to You”
rows.text: Oaths: do not swear at all — let your yes be yes and your no be no; anything more is from evil
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Again, ye have heard that it hath been said by them of old time, Thou shalt not forswear thyself, but shalt perform un…

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Antitheses — “You Have Heard… But I Say to You”
rows.text: Enemies: love, pray for, and do good to enemies — be perfect as the Father is perfect
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Ye have heard that it hath been said, Thou shalt love thy neighbour, and hate thine enemy. But I say unto you, Love yo…

rows.text: Do not take oaths at all — not by heaven, earth, Jerusalem, or your own head; let your yes be yes and your no be no
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Again, ye have heard that it hath been said by them of old time, Thou shalt not forswear thyself, but shalt perform un…

rows.text: I am the way, the truth, and the life — he was born and came into the world to bear witness to the truth; everyone on the side of truth listens to him
rows.quotePreview: I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.

rows.text: You will know the truth and the truth will set you free; if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed
rows.quotePreview: And ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free. If the Son therefore shall make you free, ye shall be fr…

rows.text: Beware of false prophets in sheep's clothing — you will know them by their fruits; a good tree cannot bear bad fruit
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Beware of false prophets, which come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly they are ravening wolves. Ye shall know …

rows.text: Beware of false christs and false prophets who perform great signs to deceive, if possible, even the elect
rows.quotePreview: Then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, or there; believe it not. For there shall arise false Christs, a…

rows.text: Your sins are forgiven — the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins
rows.quotePreview: Son, be of good cheer; thy sins be forgiven thee. Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts? For whether is easier, to say,…

rows.text: Forgive not seven times but seventy-seven / seventy times seven — without limit
rows.quotePreview: I say not unto thee, Until seven times: but, Until seventy times seven.

rows.text: Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do
rows.quotePreview: Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.

rows.text: Leave your gift at the altar — first be reconciled to your brother, then come and offer it
rows.quotePreview: Therefore if thou bring thy gift to the altar, and there rememberest that thy brother hath ought against thee; Leave the…

rows.text: From the beginning God made them male and female — what God has joined, let no one separate; Moses permitted divorce only because of hardness of heart
rows.quotePreview: Have ye not read, that he which made them at the beginning made them male and female, And said, For this cause shall a m…

rows.text: In the resurrection, people neither marry nor are given in marriage — they are like angels
rows.quotePreview: Ye do err, not knowing the scriptures, nor the power of God. For in the resurrection they neither marry, nor are given i…

rows.text: Do not store up earthly treasure — store up in heaven; where your treasure is, your heart will be also
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth, where moth and rust doth corrupt, and where thieves break through and …

rows.text: No one can serve two masters — you cannot serve both God and money
rows.quotePreview: ¶ No man can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, an…

rows.text: Woe to the rich and full — they have received their consolation
rows.quotePreview: But woe unto you that are rich! for ye have received your consolation. Woe unto you that are full! for ye shall hunger. …

rows.text: It is hard for the rich to enter the kingdom — easier for a camel through a needle's eye; with God all things are possible
rows.quotePreview: Verily I say unto you, That a rich man shall hardly enter into the kingdom of heaven. And again I say unto you, It is ea…

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Blessing on the Poor and Woe to the Rich
rows.text: Pronounces woe on the rich, full, and those who laugh now — they have already received their reward
rows.quotePreview: But woe unto you that are rich! for ye have received your consolation. Woe unto you that are full! for ye shall hunger. …

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Judgment of the Nations — Sheep and Goats
rows.text: The Son of Man separates all nations as a shepherd separates sheep from goats — those who served the hungry, thirsty, stranger, naked, sick, and imprisoned served him
rows.quotePreview: ¶ When the Son of man shall come in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then shall he sit upon the throne of hi…

rows.text: Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men — come, follow me
rows.quotePreview: Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.

rows.text: Foxes have holes and birds have nests — the Son of Man has no place to lay his head; let the dead bury their own dead
rows.quotePreview: The foxes have holes, and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head. Follow me;…

rows.text: The tower builder and the king going to war — count the cost before you begin
rows.quotePreview: For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost, whether he have sufficient …

rows.text: A good tree bears good fruit; a bad tree cannot — by their fruits you shall know them; every fruitless tree is cut down
rows.quotePreview: Even so every good tree bringeth forth good fruit; but a corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit. A good tree cannot brin…

rows.text: He who abides in him bears much fruit — apart from him you can do nothing; the Father is glorified by much fruit
rows.quotePreview: I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without…

rows.text: Teaches that those who lose their life for him keep it — and even with a troubled soul, he chose this hour to glorify the Father
rows.quotePreview: He that loveth his life shall lose it; and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal. If any…

rows.text: Promises that those who endure to the end will be saved — and the faithful unto death receive the crown of life
rows.quotePreview: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake: but he that endureth to the end shall be saved.

rows.text: Blessed are those persecuted for righteousness — blessed when people revile and persecute; rejoice, for great is the reward in heaven
rows.quotePreview: Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness’ sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. Blessed are ye, when…

rows.text: Tells the disciples the world will persecute them because it has rejected him and the Father — fulfilling the scripture 'they hated me without cause'
rows.quotePreview: But all these things will they do unto you for my name’s sake, because they know not him that sent me. If I had not come…

rows.text: Anyone who would follow must deny himself, take up his cross, and follow — whoever saves his life will lose it; whoever loses it for his sake will save it
rows.quotePreview: If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me. For whosoever will save his l…

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Seven Woes to Scribes and Pharisees (Matthew 23)
rows.text: Woe: they are like whitewashed tombs — beautiful outside, full of dead bones within
rows.quotePreview: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are like unto whited sepulchres, which indeed appear beautiful o…

rows.text: It is not what goes into a person that defiles him — what defiles comes from within: evil thoughts, sexual immorality, theft, murder, slander, arrogance
rows.quotePreview: Not that which goeth into the mouth defileth a man; but that which cometh out of the mouth, this defileth a man. But tho…

rows.text: The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath — the Son of Man is Lord of it
rows.quotePreview: The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath: Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.

rows.text: On this rock he will build his church — the gates of hell shall not prevail; he gives the keys of the kingdom
rows.quotePreview: And I say also unto thee, That thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church; and the gates of hell shall no…

rows.text: If your brother sins, go privately — if he listens, you have won him; take witnesses; then tell the church; treat the unrepentant as an outsider
rows.quotePreview: ¶ Moreover if thy brother shall trespass against thee, go and tell him his fault between thee and him alone: if he shall…

rows.text: He prays that they all may be one as the Father and Son are one — that the world may believe that the Father sent him
rows.quotePreview: That they all may be one; as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee, that they also may be one in us: that the world may…

rows.text: A kingdom divided against itself cannot stand
rows.quotePreview: Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and every city or house divided against itself shall not …

rows.text: No prophet is accepted in his own country — Elijah was sent to a widow of Sidon, and only Naaman the Syrian was cleansed
rows.quotePreview: Ye will surely say unto me this proverb, Physician, heal thyself: whatsoever we have heard done in Capernaum, do also he…

rows.subcategoryTitle: Instructions for the Twelve and the Seventy
rows.text: Go and proclaim: the kingdom of heaven is at hand; heal the sick, raise the dead, cast out demons — freely give
rows.quotePreview: And as ye go, preach, saying, The kingdom of heaven is at hand. Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, cast …

rows.text: What is whispered, proclaim on the housetops; do not fear those who kill the body — fear him who can destroy both soul and body
rows.quotePreview: What I tell you in darkness, that speak ye in light: and what ye hear in the ear, that preach ye upon the housetops. And…

rows.subcategoryTitle: Visions and Commissions to the Apostles
rows.text: In a vision sends Ananias to Saul — a chosen instrument to carry his name before Gentiles, kings, and Israel
rows.quotePreview: Ananias. Arise, and go into the street which is called Straight, and enquire in the house of Judas for one called Saul, …

rows.subcategoryTitle: Visions and Commissions to the Apostles
rows.text: The Lord stands by Paul in his Jerusalem custody and encourages him, confirming that just as he has testified about him in Jerusalem he must also testify in Rome.
rows.quotePreview: Be of good cheer, Paul: for as thou hast testified of me in Jerusalem, so must thou bear witness also at Rome.

rows.text: Foretells his crucifixion at Passover — after two days is the feast, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified
rows.quotePreview: Ye know that after two days is the feast of the passover, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified.

rows.text: Defends the woman who anointed him — she has done a good work, pouring ointment for my burial, and shall be remembered wherever the gospel is preached
rows.quotePreview: Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath wrought a good work upon me. For ye have the poor always with you; but me ye have…

rows.text: Pilate would have no power against him at all unless it were given from above; therefore the one who delivered him to Pilate has the greater sin.
rows.quotePreview: Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto…

rows.text: Put your finger here, see his hands, reach into his side — do not disbelieve but believe; blessed are those who have not seen and yet believe
rows.quotePreview: Reach hither thy finger, and behold my hands; and reach hither thy hand, and thrust it into my side: and be not faithles…

rows.text: From the shore he asks the fishermen if they have any meat, and tells them to cast the net on the right side of the ship, where they will find a catch.
rows.quotePreview: Children, have ye any meat? Cast the net on the right side of the ship, and ye shall find.

rows.text: All authority has been given — go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing and teaching them to observe all his commands; he is with them always, to the end of the age
rows.quotePreview: All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth. ¶ Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name …

rows.text: Wait in Jerusalem for the promised Spirit — not to know times and seasons, but to receive power and be his witnesses to the ends of the earth
rows.quotePreview: but wait for the promise of the Father, which, ye have heard of me. For John truly baptized with water; but ye shall be …

rows.text: The abomination of desolation in the holy place — let those in Judea flee; then great tribulation such as has never been
rows.quotePreview: When ye therefore shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, stand in the holy place, (wh…

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Return of the Son of Man
rows.text: The Son of Man will come on the clouds with power and great glory — his angels will gather the elect from the four winds
rows.quotePreview: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn, and they s…

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Return of the Son of Man
rows.text: One will be taken and one left
rows.quotePreview: Then shall two be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left. Two women shall be grinding at the mill; the…

rows.text: The ten virgins — five wise with oil prepared, five foolish who ran out; the door was shut — be ready
rows.quotePreview: Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins, which took their lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegr…

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Fig Tree and the Unknown Hour
rows.text: Promises the Twelve will sit on twelve thrones judging the tribes of Israel when the Son of Man reigns
rows.quotePreview: Verily I say unto you, That ye which have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit in the throne o…

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Certainty and Scope of Judgment
rows.text: It will be more tolerable for Sodom than for those who reject his messengers; all in the tombs will come out — the good to life, the evil to judgment
rows.quotePreview: Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for th…

rows.subcategoryTitle: The Certainty and Scope of Judgment
rows.text: Nineveh and the Queen of the South will rise in judgment against this generation — for a greater than Jonas and Solomon is here
rows.quotePreview: The men of Nineveh shall rise in judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: because they repented at the preac…

rows.text: Outer darkness — weeping and gnashing of teeth; Gehenna of fire
rows.quotePreview: But the children of the kingdom shall be cast out into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

rows.text: Depart from me, you cursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil — eternal punishment versus eternal life
rows.quotePreview: Then shall he say also unto them on the left hand, Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the de…

rows.subcategoryTitle: Ephesus — The Church That Left Its First Love
rows.text: Commends their toil, endurance, and testing of false apostles — but they have abandoned their first love; remember, repent, and return to those works
rows.quotePreview: I know thy works, and thy labour, and thy patience, and how thou canst not bear them which are evil: and thou hast tried…

rows.text: To the one who conquers: the crown of life; will not be hurt by the second death
rows.quotePreview: Fear none of those things which thou shalt suffer: behold, the devil shall cast some of you into prison, that ye may be …

rows.text: Addresses Smyrna as the First and the Last, who was dead and is alive
rows.quotePreview: And unto the angel of the church in Smyrna write; These things saith the first and the last, which was dead, and is aliv…

rows.text: You have held fast my name and not denied my faith — but some hold the teaching of Balaam and practice sexual immorality; repent or I will war against them
rows.quotePreview: I know thy works, and where thou dwellest, even where Satan’s seat is: and thou holdest fast my name, and hast not denie…

rows.text: To the one who conquers: hidden manna and a white stone with a new name
rows.quotePreview: He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches; To him that overcometh will I give to eat of …

rows.text: You have a reputation of being alive but are dead — wake up and strengthen what remains; remember, keep it, and repent; if not, I will come like a thief
rows.quotePreview: And unto the angel of the church in Sardis write; These things saith he that hath the seven Spirits of God, and the seve…

rows.text: I have set before you an open door no one can shut; you have kept my word and not denied my name; I will keep you from the hour of trial
rows.quotePreview: I know thy works: behold, I have set before thee an open door, and no man can shut it: for thou hast a little strength, …

rows.text: I am coming soon — hold fast so no one seizes your crown; to the one who conquers: a pillar in God's temple with the name of God and the new Jerusalem
rows.quotePreview: Behold, I come quickly: hold that fast which thou hast, that no man take thy crown. Him that overcometh will I make a pi…

rows.text: Behold, I stand at the door and knock — if anyone opens I will come in; to the one who conquers: I will grant to sit with me on my throne
rows.quotePreview: Behold, I stand at the door, and knock: if any man hear my voice, and open the door, I will come in to him, and will sup…

rows.subcategoryTitle: Introduction to the Seven Churches — The Vision of Christ
rows.text: The risen Jesus commands John to write in a book what he sees and send it to the seven churches: Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.
rows.quotePreview: I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last: What thou seest, write in a book, and send it unto the seven churches whic…
